channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Home to centuries of heritage, Sudan's biggest museum is looted and wrecked by a 2-year war
item.description: The exhibition halls of Sudan's biggest museum were once filled with statues and relics from centuries of ancient civilizations.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about Ecuador's reelected President Noboa and his plans to fight crime
item.description: Daniel Noboa stunned voters in 2023 when he won a snap election for a 16-month presidency after only a brief stint as a lawmaker and with no established political machinery.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano And Gonzalo Solano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian missiles hit Ukrainian city of Sumy during Palm Sunday celebrations, killing more than 30
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missiles struck the heart of the Ukrainian city of Sumy as people gathered to celebrate Palm Sunday, killing at least 34 people, officials said, in the second large-scale attack to claim civilian lives in just over a week. The two ballistic missiles hit around 10:15 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China's Xi says there are 'no winners' in a tariff war as he visits Southeast Asia
item.description: China’s leader Xi Jinping started a week of diplomacy in Southeast Asia with a visit to Vietnam on Monday, signaling China’s commitment to global trade, just after U.S. President Donald Trump upended the global economy with his latest tariffs moves.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Aniruddha Ghosal, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 6 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen’s rebel-held capital killed at least six people and wounded 26 overnight.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mario Vargas Llosa, Peruvian author and Nobel literature laureate, dies at 89
item.description: Peruvian author Mario Vargas Llosa, Nobel literature laureate and a giant of Latin American letters for many decades, has died, his son said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Franklin Briceño And Ciarán Giles, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 50 people killed in east Congo as government and Rwanda-backed rebels trade blame
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 50 people were killed in weekend attacks in Congo’s conflict-battered east, authorities said Saturday. The government traded blame with Rwanda-backed rebels over who was responsible for the violence that quickly escalated the conflict in the region.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and JEAN-YVES KAMALE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria's new leader makes his first visit to the United Arab Emirates
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa made his first visit Sunday to the United Arab Emirates, whose leaders have been circumspect about the new leadership in Damascus in the four months since the fall of former President Bashar Assad in a lightning rebel offensive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gabon’s coup leader Oligui Nguema elected president with 90% of votes cast, provisional results show
item.description: LIBREVILLE, Gabon (AP) — Gen. Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema, Gabon’s interim president who staged a 2023 coup that ended a decadeslong political dynasty, has been elected president, according to provisional results announced by the country’s interior ministry.
item.dc:creator: YVES LAURENT GOMA and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Convalescing Pope Francis opens Holy Week with in-person greeting in St. Peter's Square
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A convalescing Pope Francis greeted the crowd in St. Peter’s Square on Palm Sunday, wishing more than 20,000 faithful a “Happy Palm Sunday, Happy Holy Week," in yet another reassuring public sign of his recovery from a life-threatening battle with double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Central Myanmar shaken by new quake in one of the biggest aftershocks since March 28 temblor
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A magnitude 5.5 earthquake struck on Sunday morning near Meiktila, a small city in central Myanmar, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic on Saturday demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Trump's backing Israel pushes deeper into Gaza as pressure builds for hostage deal
item.description: An Israeli official says military operations and hostage talks are fully coordinated with the U.S., as officials remain optimistic about a deal. Israel continues its advance in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico to send water to Texas amid Trump tariff threats
item.description: Mexico will facilitate an “immediate” delivery of water to farmers in Texas to help shore up resources amid President Trump’s tariff threats, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade on Friday, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's divisions on display as presidential candidates bicker in a live TV debate
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Bickering and chaos overshadowed a presidential debate in Poland on Friday evening as eight candidates met to discuss existential matters for this country on NATO's eastern flank — including fears of Russia over its war in Ukraine and concerns about President Donald Trump’s com
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: European countries vow billions in military support for Ukraine as US envoy meets Putin
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European countries vowed Friday to sends billions of dollars in further funding to help Ukraine keep fighting Russia’s invasion, as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 18 dead, 120 injured in Dominican Republican nightclub rooftop collapse
item.description: 18 people died and more than 120 were injured when a roof fell at a nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Harry appeals the loss of his UK security detail
item.description: Harry lost his government-funded protection in February 2020 after he stepped down from his role and moved to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9
